videos.title: RAMageddon has come for Apple | The Vergecast
videos.description: Meta's business is doing just fine. But Meta as a company, and Meta as a series of products? That is, uh, messier. David and Nilay discuss the company's ongoing desire to be relevant and cool, the unceasing importance of Instagram, and why it makes perfect sense that Facebook would clone Polymarket. After that, the hosts talk about Apple's huge price increases, and the ways in which RAMageddon might change the gadget market forever. For years, the dream of a do-everything workstation required a trade-off between raw horsepower and the battery life needed for true portability. The 2026 Dell XPS laptop changes that conversation. From its refined CNC machined aluminum chassis to its 2.8K tandem OLED display—not to mention smart thermals and a long battery life—every detail is engineered for those who need productivity on the go. videos.title: How to train your data | The Vergecast
videos.description: Training data is the raw material of the AI industry. Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, and the rest are built on top of oceans of stuff. What is that stuff? Books. Blog posts. YouTube videos. Reddit comments. All of it and more, in virtually incomprehensible quantities. Alex Reisner, a staff writer at The Atlantic who has been investigating training data, explains how AI companies get all this data, why they'd really prefer you not know what's in it, and whether training data could ever be a fair trade.

videos.title: Google's new speaker and your smart home questions | The Vergecast
videos.description:
Google is shipping its first smart speaker in six years, and we're starting to test it. The Verge's Jennifer Pattison Tuohy joins the show to explain why the Home Speaker matters, whether Google actually cares about the smart home, and more. Then, she helps answer a few questions from the Vergecast Hotline (call 866-VERGE11 or email vergecast@theverge.com!) about the power of Ikea and the future of your thermostat.

0:00 - Intro
0:41 - 90 Seconds on The Verge (news roundup)
2:36 - Smart home Q&A
